findings.title: HTTPS Not Enforced (HSTS Missing)
findings.description: Your site lacks HTTP Strict Transport Security. Browsers won't automatically force HTTPS connections, leaving visitors vulnerable to downgrade attacks where an attacker intercepts the connection before HTTPS is established.
findings.impact: Man-in-the-middle attacks, session hijacking on public Wi-Fi.
findings.recommendation:
Summary: Add: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload — This tells browsers to always use HTTPS for the next year

Steps:

1. Review the recommendation and identify the responsible system (web server, firewall, application).

2. Apply the recommended change in a staging environment first.

3. Deploy to production and verify the change with a follow-up scan.

Example commands:

Certbot (nginx): sudo certbot --nginx -d yourdomain.com

Difficulty: High

Estimated time: 30-120 minutes

Tip: Test changes in staging and monitor after deployment.
findings.evidence: Header "strict-transport-security" not found in server response

findings.title: Content Security Policy (CSP) Not Set
findings.description: Without CSP, your site is vulnerable to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks. Malicious scripts can be injected and run in your visitors' browsers, stealing session cookies, passwords, and personal data.
findings.impact: XSS attacks can steal user sessions, credentials, and personal data.
findings.recommendation:
Summary: Start with: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' — Then customize for your CDN/analytics needs

Steps:

1. Start with a restrictive CSP: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self';

2. Allowlist any external domains you explicitly use (CDNs, analytics).

3. Deploy in report-only mode first to tune the policy.

Difficulty: Medium

Estimated time: 15-60 minutes

Tip: Test changes in staging and monitor after deployment.
findings.evidence: Header "content-security-policy" not found in server response

findings.title: Clickjacking Protection Missing
findings.description: Your website can be embedded inside an invisible iframe on a malicious website. Attackers overlay fake buttons to trick users into performing actions like making purchases or changing account settings without knowing.
findings.impact: Clickjacking attacks stealing user actions and data.
findings.recommendation:
Summary: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY (or SAMEORIGIN if you embed your own pages in iframes)

Steps:

1. Review the recommendation and identify the responsible system (web server, firewall, application).

2. Apply the recommended change in a staging environment first.

3. Deploy to production and verify the change with a follow-up scan.

Difficulty: Medium

Estimated time: 15-60 minutes

Tip: Test changes in staging and monitor after deployment.
findings.evidence: Header "x-frame-options" not found in server response

findings.title: MIME Type Sniffing Not Prevented
findings.description: Without this header, browsers try to "guess" file types. Attackers can upload files with misleading extensions (e.g., an image that's actually JavaScript) that browsers will execute.
findings.impact: MIME confusion attacks enabling script execution.
findings.recommendation:
Summary: Add: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff — Simple one-liner that prevents this class of attack

Steps:

1. Review the recommendation and identify the responsible system (web server, firewall, application).

2. Apply the recommended change in a staging environment first.

3. Deploy to production and verify the change with a follow-up scan.

Difficulty: Low

Estimated time: 5-30 minutes

Tip: Test changes in staging and monitor after deployment.
findings.evidence: Header "x-content-type-options" not found in server response

findings.title: Referrer Information Leaking to Third Parties
findings.description: When users click links on your site, the full URL (including any sensitive parameters like user IDs or session tokens) is sent to the destination website in the HTTP Referer header.
findings.impact: Privacy leak of URL structure and user data to external sites.
findings.recommendation:
Summary: Add: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin — Sends only the origin, not full URL, to external sites

Steps:

1. Review the recommendation and identify the responsible system (web server, firewall, application).

2. Apply the recommended change in a staging environment first.

3. Deploy to production and verify the change with a follow-up scan.

Difficulty: Low

Estimated time: 5-30 minutes

Tip: Test changes in staging and monitor after deployment.
findings.evidence: Header "referrer-policy" not found in server response

findings.title: Browser Feature Permissions Unrestricted
findings.description: Your site hasn't restricted browser features like camera, microphone, geolocation, and USB. Malicious third-party scripts on your page could silently request these permissions.
findings.impact: Malicious scripts could abuse device features.
findings.recommendation:
Summary: Add: Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=() — Disables features your site doesn't need

Steps:

1. Review the recommendation and identify the responsible system (web server, firewall, application).

2. Apply the recommended change in a staging environment first.

3. Deploy to production and verify the change with a follow-up scan.

Difficulty: Low

Estimated time: 5-30 minutes

Tip: Test changes in staging and monitor after deployment.
findings.evidence: Header "permissions-policy" not found in server response

findings.title: Cross-Origin Isolation Not Configured
findings.description: COEP is not set. This prevents enabling cross-origin isolation which protects against Spectre-style side-channel attacks.
findings.impact: Side-channel memory attacks in shared environments.
findings.recommendation:
Summary: Add: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp for high-security applications

Steps:

1. Review the recommendation and identify the responsible system (web server, firewall, application).

2. Apply the recommended change in a staging environment first.

3. Deploy to production and verify the change with a follow-up scan.

Difficulty: Low

Estimated time: 5-30 minutes

Tip: Test changes in staging and monitor after deployment.
findings.evidence: Header "cross-origin-embedder-policy" not found in server response

findings.title: HTTP Version Accessible Without Redirect
findings.description: While HTTPS works, users who visit http:// are not automatically redirected to the secure version. First-time visitors or bookmarks using HTTP are unprotected.
findings.impact: Users connecting via HTTP are not protected.
findings.recommendation:
Summary: Configure a permanent 301 redirect from all HTTP URLs to HTTPS at the web server level

Steps:

1. Review the recommendation and identify the responsible system (web server, firewall, application).

2. Apply the recommended change in a staging environment first.

3. Deploy to production and verify the change with a follow-up scan.

Example commands:

Certbot (nginx): sudo certbot --nginx -d yourdomain.com

Difficulty: Medium

Estimated time: 15-60 minutes

Tip: Test changes in staging and monitor after deployment.
